summary: Wallet/credential stealer with hardcoded C2 in mnemonic-safety-check
details: Package name advertises a wallet seed-phrase safety tool, and the shipped code matches the wallet/credential-stealer fingerprint. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration and POSTs collected data over HTTPS, with multiple curl/ping shell-outs. scanner.js hardcodes the C2 endpoint https://ddjidd564.github.io (referenced at lines 161 and 323) and uses https.request/https.get + child_process.execSync (including `whoami`) to gather host identity (os.hostname(), username, cwd) and exfiltrate it. build-verifier.js similarly bundles os/fs/https/child_process with hostname/username/cwd collection and an execSync('docker push...') path. The combined fingerprints — hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 on a GitHub Pages host paired with browser/wallet-scanner-shaped enumeration of the user's filesystem and host identifiers — are unambiguously a credential/wallet stealer, not a legitimate mnemonic-safety utility. Installing or loading this package will exfiltrate installer host data and likely wallet/seed material to the attacker endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
